sourceMovie.title: True Lies
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit True Lies for the same reason it broke through in 1994: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. James Cameron keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: RED
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If True Lies clicked for you, RED (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Safe House
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If True Lies clicked for you, Safe House (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after True Lies?
faq.answer: Try RED, Safe House, Die Another Day—each shares DNA with True Lies while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is True Lies?
faq.answer: True Lies (1994) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is True Lies worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
